title.raw: 卷十六 鄧寇列傳 Volume 16: Biographies of Deng, Kou

sentences.zh: 鄧禹字仲華，南陽新野人也。

translations.literal: Deng Yu, courtesy name Zhonghua, was a man of Xinye in Nanyang.
translations.idiomatic: Deng Yu, whose courtesy name was Zhonghua, came from Xinye in Nanyang commandery.

sentences.zh: 年十三，能誦詩，受業長安。

translations.literal: At age thirteen he could recite the Odes and received instruction in Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: At thirteen he had already memorized the Classic of Odes and pursued his studies in the capital.

sentences.zh: 時光武亦遊學京師，禹年雖幼，而見光武知非常人，遂相親附。

translations.literal: At that time Guangwu also roamed to study in the capital; though Yu was young, when he saw Guangwu he knew he was no ordinary man, and thereupon drew close and attached himself to him.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu was then studying in the capital as well. Deng Yu was still a boy, but one look at Guangwu convinced him that this was no ordinary man, and he attached himself to him.

sentences.zh: 數年歸家。

translations.literal: After several years he returned home.
translations.idiomatic: Some years later he went home.

sentences.zh: 及漢兵起，更始立，豪傑多薦舉禹，禹不肯從。

translations.literal: When the Han troops rose and Gengshi was enthroned, many bold men recommended Yu, but Yu was unwilling to follow.
translations.idiomatic: When the Han armies rose and Liu Xuan took the throne as Emperor Gengshi, local leaders repeatedly urged Deng Yu to serve; he refused.

sentences.zh: 及聞光武安集河北，即杖策北渡，追及於鄴。

translations.literal: When he heard that Guangwu was pacifying and gathering Hebei, he immediately took staff and whip, crossed north, and overtook him at Ye.
translations.idiomatic: Learning that Guangwu was consolidating his position north of the Yellow River, he took his riding crop, rode north, and caught up with him at Ye.

sentences.zh: 光武見之甚歡，謂曰：『我得專封拜，生遠來，寧欲仕乎？

translations.literal: Guangwu saw him and was greatly pleased, saying to him: 'I alone may confer fiefs and appointments; you have come from afar—do you wish to take office?'
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu was delighted to see him and said, 'I alone can grant titles and posts. You have traveled a long way—are you looking for an appointment?'

sentences.zh: 』禹曰：『不願也。

translations.literal: Yu said: 'I do not wish it.'
translations.idiomatic: Deng Yu answered, 'No, I am not.'

sentences.zh: 』光武曰：『即如是，何欲為？

translations.literal: Guangwu said: 'If it is thus, what do you wish to do?'
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu asked, 'Then what do you want?'

sentences.zh: 』禹曰：『但願明公威德加於四海，禹得效其尺寸，垂功名於竹帛耳。

translations.literal: Yu said: 'I only wish that the illustrious lord's awe-inspiring virtue may extend to the four seas, and that Yu may offer his slight measure of strength and leave his name and merit on bamboo and silk.'
translations.idiomatic: Deng Yu replied, 'I only hope your lordship's power and virtue will spread across the realm, and that I may contribute whatever small service I can, so that my name might be recorded for posterity.'

sentences.zh: 』光武笑，因留宿閑語。

translations.literal: Guangwu laughed and thereupon kept him overnight in relaxed conversation.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu laughed, kept him as a guest that night, and they talked at leisure.

sentences.zh: 禹進說曰：『更始雖都關西，今山東未安，赤眉、青犢之屬，動以萬數，三輔假號，往往群聚。

translations.literal: Yu advanced counsel, saying: 'Though Gengshi now has his capital west of the passes, east of the mountains is not yet secure; bands such as the Red Eyebrows and the Green Calves move in numbers of ten thousands; in the Three Adjuncts many falsely assume titles and often gather in groups.'
translations.idiomatic: Deng Yu urged him, saying, 'Gengshi may hold the Guanzhong heartland, but the region east of the mountains is still unsettled. The Red Eyebrows, the Green Calves, and their like field tens of thousands of men. In the Three Adjuncts, impostors claiming rank are everywhere, clustering into bands.'

sentences.zh: 更始既未有所挫，而不自聽斷，諸將皆庸人屈起，志在財幣，爭用威力，朝夕自快而已，非有忠良明智，深慮遠圖，欲尊主安民者也。

translations.literal: Gengshi has not yet suffered any setback, yet does not himself listen and decide; the generals are all mediocre men risen from humble stations, their aim set on wealth and coin, competing to use force and power, morning and evening seeking their own pleasure only—they are not loyal and good, wise and enlightened, with deep plans and far-reaching designs, wishing to honor the ruler and secure the people.
translations.idiomatic: Gengshi has not been checked by any reverse, yet he will not take decisions himself. His generals are petty men who have clawed their way up; they care only for plunder, vie to throw their weight around, and live from day to day for their own amusement. There is no loyal wisdom among them, no long view of how to exalt the sovereign and bring peace to the common people.

sentences.zh: 四方分崩離析，形勢可見。

translations.literal: The four quarters are splitting apart and falling to pieces; the situation can be seen.
translations.idiomatic: The empire is coming apart; the trend is plain to see.

sentences.zh: 明公雖建藩輔之功，猶恐無所成立。

translations.literal: Though the illustrious lord has achieved merit as a supporting vassal, still I fear there will be nothing by which to establish success.
translations.idiomatic: You have done great service as his supporter, yet I fear that will not be enough to build something lasting.

sentences.zh: 於今之計，莫如延攬英雄，務悅民心，立高祖之業，救萬民之命。

translations.literal: In the plan for the present, nothing is better than gathering in heroes, striving to please the people's hearts, establishing the enterprise of the High Ancestor, and saving the lives of the myriad people.
translations.idiomatic: For now, nothing beats winning over able men, earning the people's goodwill, reviving the enterprise of Emperor Gaozu of Han, and rescuing the common people from their plight.

sentences.zh: 以公而慮天下，不足定也。

translations.literal: With you, illustrious lord, giving your mind to All-under-Heaven, nothing is wanting for its pacification.
translations.idiomatic: A man of your caliber need only bend his will to the empire—its pacification is assured.

sentences.zh: 』光武大悅，因令左右號禹曰鄧將軍。

translations.literal: Guangwu was greatly pleased and thereupon ordered those at his side to style Yu General Deng.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu was deeply pleased and told his attendants to address Deng Yu as General Deng.

sentences.zh: 常宿止於中，與定計議。

translations.literal: He often lodged and stopped in the headquarters, with him settling plans and deliberations.
translations.idiomatic: Deng Yu was often quartered in headquarters, where he and Guangwu hammered out strategy together.

sentences.zh: 及王郎起兵，光武自薊至信都，使禹發奔命，得數千人，令自將之，別攻拔樂陽。

translations.literal: When Wang Lang raised troops, Guangwu went from Ji to Xindu, sent Yu to issue emergency call-ups, obtained several thousand men, ordered him to command them himself, and separately attacked and took Leyang.
translations.idiomatic: When Wang Lang rebelled, Guangwu marched from Ji to Xindu. He had Deng Yu raise emergency levies, gathered several thousand men, put Deng Yu in independent command, and sent him to storm and seize Leyang.

sentences.zh: 從至廣阿，光武舍城樓上，披輿地圖，指示禹曰：『天下郡國如是，今始乃得其一。

translations.literal: Following him to Guang'a, Guangwu lodged on the city tower, spread open the map of the realm, and pointed, saying to Yu: 'Commanderies and kingdoms of the realm are like this; now we have only obtained one of them.'
translations.idiomatic: He accompanied Guangwu to Guang'a. Guangwu sat on the city wall with the empire's map spread before him, traced the commanderies and kingdoms, and said to Deng Yu, 'The realm looks like this—and we hold only a single corner of it.'

sentences.zh: 子前言以吾慮天下不足定，何也？

translations.literal: You earlier said that with my considering the realm it was not enough to settle it—why?'
translations.idiomatic: You once told me that if I set my mind on the whole realm, I could not hope to pacify it. What did you mean by that?'

sentences.zh: 』禹曰：『方今海內淆亂，人思明君，猶赤子之慕慈母。

translations.literal: Yu said: 'At present within the seas it is confused and chaotic; people long for a clear-sighted ruler, like a newborn babe yearning for its mother.'
translations.idiomatic: Deng Yu replied, 'The empire is in turmoil. The people are crying out for a true ruler, as an infant longs for its mother.'

sentences.zh: 古之興者，在德薄厚，不以大小。

translations.literal: Those who rose in antiquity lay in whether virtue was thin or thick, not in whether they were large or small.
translations.idiomatic: The founders of old rose or fell on the depth of their virtue, not on the size of the territory they started with.

sentences.zh: 』光武悅。

translations.literal: Guangwu was pleased.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu was satisfied.

sentences.zh: 時任使諸將，多訪於禹，禹每有所舉者，皆當其才，光武以為知人。

translations.literal: At that time when appointing the generals to missions, he often consulted Yu; whenever Yu recommended someone, all matched their talents, and Guangwu considered him one who knew men.
translations.idiomatic: Whenever Guangwu dispatched his generals, he often asked Deng Yu's advice. Every man Deng Yu put forward proved equal to the task, and Guangwu concluded that he had a gift for reading character.

sentences.zh: 使別將騎，與蓋延等擊銅馬於清陽。

translations.literal: He sent separate command of cavalry, with Gai Yan and others to strike the Bronze Horse at Qingyang.
translations.idiomatic: He gave him a separate cavalry command to join Gai Yan and the others in attacking the Bronze Horse band at Qingyang.

sentences.zh: 延等先至，戰不利，還保城，為賊所圍。

translations.literal: Yan and the others arrived first; the battle went badly, they returned to protect the city, and were surrounded by the bandits.
translations.idiomatic: Gai Yan's detachment arrived first, was beaten in the fight, fell back to hold the city, and found itself besieged by the rebels.

sentences.zh: 禹遂進與戰，破之，生獲其大將。

translations.literal: Yu thereupon advanced and fought them, defeated them, and alive captured their great general.
translations.idiomatic: Deng Yu advanced to give battle, broke the enemy, and took their chief general alive.

sentences.zh: 從光武追賊至蒲陽，連大克獲，北州略定。

translations.literal: Following Guangwu in pursuit of the bandits to Puyang, repeatedly great captures were made, and the northern provinces were broadly settled.
translations.idiomatic: He followed Guangwu in pursuit of the rebels to Puyang, won victory after victory, and helped bring the northern provinces under control.

sentences.zh: 及赤眉西入關，更始使定國上公王匡、襄邑王成丹、抗威將軍劉均及諸將，分據河東、弘農以拒之。

translations.literal: When the Red Eyebrows went west into the passes, Gengshi sent the Duke Who Settles the State Wang Kuang, the King of Xiangyi Cheng Dan, the General Who Resists Might Liu Jun, and the generals to divide and hold Hedong and Hongnong to resist them.
translations.idiomatic: When the Red Eyebrows marched west into Guanzhong, Gengshi sent Wang Kuang the Duke Who Settles the State, Cheng Dan the king of Xiangyi, Liu Jun the General Who Resists Might, and other officers to divide their forces between Hedong and Hongnong and block the advance.

sentences.zh: 赤眉眾大集，王匡等莫能當。

translations.literal: The Red Eyebrows gathered in great mass; Wang Kuang and the others could not withstand them.
translations.idiomatic: The Red Eyebrows massed in overwhelming strength, and Wang Kuang's line could not hold them.

sentences.zh: 光武籌赤眉必破長安，欲乘釁並關中，而方自事山東，未知所寄，以禹沈深有大度，故授以西討之略。

translations.literal: Guangwu calculated that the Red Eyebrows would surely break Chang'an, wished to exploit the breach and annex Guanzhong, but was then occupied with matters east of the mountains and did not know to whom to entrust it; because Yu was deep and steady with great capacity, he therefore invested him with the strategy of the western expedition.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu reckoned the Red Eyebrows would inevitably take Chang'an and wanted to slip through the opening and seize Guanzhong for himself, yet he was tied down east of the mountains and had no one to whom he could safely delegate the western campaign. Deng Yu's gravity and breadth of mind convinced him to entrust the plan to Deng Yu.

sentences.zh: 乃拜為前將軍持節，中分麾下精兵二萬人，遣西入關，令自選偏裨以下可與俱者。

translations.literal: Thereupon he was appointed General of the Van with credentials, divided in half the crack troops under his command—twenty thousand men—and sent west into the passes, ordering him to choose from the deputy commanders down those who could go with him.
translations.idiomatic: He therefore commissioned Deng Yu as General of the Van with imperial credentials, detached twenty thousand picked troops from his own army, and sent them west into the passes, leaving Deng Yu to choose which subordinate officers would accompany him.

sentences.zh: 於是以韓歆為軍師，李文、李春、程慮為祭酒，馮愔為積弩將軍，樊崇為驍騎將軍，宗歆為車騎將軍，鄧尋為建威將軍，耿訢為赤眉將軍，左於為軍師將軍，引而西。

translations.literal: Thereupon Han Xin was made army director, Li Wen, Li Chun, and Cheng Lü were libationers, Feng Yin was General Who Accumulates Crossbows, Fan Chong was General of the Valiant Cavalry, Zong Xin was General of Chariots and Cavalry, Deng Xun was General Who Establishes Might, Geng Xin was General of the Red Eyebrows, Zuo Yu was General Who Is Army Director, and they set out west.
translations.idiomatic: Han Xin was named director of the army; Li Wen, Li Chun, and Cheng Lü became libationers; Feng Yin, general of accumulated crossbows; Fan Chong, general of valiant cavalry; Zong Xin, general of chariots and cavalry; Deng Xun, general who establishes might; Geng Xin, general of the Red Eyebrows; Zuo Yu, general who serves as army director—and the column marched west.

sentences.zh: 建武元年正月，禹自箕關將入河東，河東都尉守關不開，禹攻十日，破之，獲輜重千餘乘。

translations.literal: In the first month of the first year of Jianwu, Yu was about to enter Hedong from Jiguan; the Hedong commandery captain guarded the pass and would not open; Yu attacked ten days, broke it, and captured more than a thousand baggage carts.
translations.idiomatic: In the first month of Jianwu 1, Deng Yu advanced on Hedong through Jiguan. The commandery captain refused to yield the pass. Deng Yu assaulted it for ten days, forced it, and seized over a thousand supply wagons.

sentences.zh: 進圍安邑，數月未能下。

translations.literal: He advanced to besiege Anyi; for several months he could not take it.
translations.idiomatic: He then laid siege to Anyi but could not reduce it for months.

sentences.zh: 更始大將軍樊參將數萬人，度大陽欲攻禹，禹遣諸將逆擊於解南，大破之，斬參首。

translations.literal: Gengshi's grand general Fan Can led several tens of thousands of men, crossed Dayang intending to attack Yu; Yu sent the generals to meet and strike them south of Xie, greatly defeated them, and beheaded Can.
translations.idiomatic: Gengshi's grand general Fan Can crossed Dayang at the head of tens of thousands, intending to fall on Deng Yu. Deng Yu sent his officers to intercept them south of Xie, shattered their army, and took Fan Can's head.

sentences.zh: 於是王匡、成丹、劉均等合軍十餘萬，復共擊禹，禹軍不利，樊崇戰死。

translations.literal: Thereupon Wang Kuang, Cheng Dan, Liu Jun, and others combined armies of more than a hundred thousand and again jointly struck Yu; Yu's army fared badly, and Fan Chong died in battle.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Kuang, Cheng Dan, Liu Jun, and their allies then threw more than a hundred thousand men against him. Deng Yu's army took heavy losses, and Fan Chong was killed in the fighting.

sentences.zh: 會日幕，戰罷，軍師韓歆及諸將見兵勢已摧，皆勸禹夜去，禹不聽。

translations.literal: It happened to be dusk; battle ceased. Army director Han Xin and the generals, seeing that the army's momentum was already broken, all urged Yu to leave by night; Yu would not listen.
translations.idiomatic: Night was falling when the fighting stopped. Han Xin the army director and the other officers, seeing how badly the tide had turned, urged Deng Yu to withdraw under cover of darkness; he refused.

sentences.zh: 明日癸亥，匡等以六甲窮日不出，禹因得更理兵勒眾。

translations.literal: The next day was guihai; Kuang and the others, because it was an exhausted day in the six jia cycle, did not go out, and Yu thereby obtained further opportunity to reorder the troops and marshal the host.
translations.idiomatic: The next day was guihai, a 'spent' day in the sexagenary cycle. Wang Kuang and his colleagues would not leave their lines, which gave Deng Yu time to reorganize his battered army.

sentences.zh: 明旦，匡悉軍出攻禹，禹令軍中無得妄動；

translations.literal: At dawn Kuang brought out his entire army to attack Yu; Yu ordered that in the army none might move rashly.
translations.idiomatic: At daybreak Wang Kuang threw his whole force against Deng Yu, who ordered his men to hold their ground and not stir rashly.

sentences.zh: 既至營下，因傳發諸將鼓而並進，大破廣。

translations.literal: When they had reached beneath the enemy camp, he relayed orders to the generals; drums sounded and all advanced together, and he greatly defeated Kuang.
translations.idiomatic: Once his men were at the enemy camp, he signaled his generals; the drums rolled and every unit charged as one, shattering Wang Kuang's army.

sentences.zh: 匡等皆棄軍亡走，禹率輕騎急追，獲劉均及河東太守楊寶、持節中郎將弭韁，皆斬之，收得節六，印綬五百，兵器不可勝數，遂定河東。

translations.literal: Kuang and the others all abandoned the army and fled; Yu led light cavalry in urgent pursuit, captured Liu Jun and Hedong grand administrator Yang Bao and staff-bearing general of the Gentlemen of the Household Mi Jiang, all beheaded them, collected six credentials, five hundred seals and ribbons, weapons beyond counting, and thereupon settled Hedong.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Kuang and his allies broke and ran. Deng Yu led light horse in hot pursuit, ran down Liu Jun, Hedong Administrator Yang Bao, and Household Gentleman Mi Jiang, executed them all, took six imperial batons, five hundred seals and cords, and arms beyond reckoning, and secured Hedong.

sentences.zh: 承制拜李文為河東太守，悉更置屬縣令長以鎮撫之。

translations.literal: Acting on imperial authority he appointed Li Wen Hedong grand administrator, wholly replaced the county magistrates and chiefs of the subordinate counties to pacify and soothe them.
translations.idiomatic: Acting with delegated authority he named Li Wen administrator of Hedong, replaced every county magistrate in the commandery, and set them to restoring order.

sentences.zh: 是月，光武即位於鄗，使使者持節拜禹為大司徒。

translations.literal: That month Guangwu ascended the throne at Hao and sent an envoy with credentials to appoint Yu as grand minister of education.
translations.idiomatic: That same month Guangwu took the throne at Hao and dispatched an envoy with imperial credentials to appoint Deng Yu grand minister of education.

sentences.zh: 策曰：『制詔前將軍禹：深執忠孝，與朕謀謨帷幄，決勝千里。

translations.literal: The rescript said: 'Ordered edict to General of the Van Yu: deeply holding loyalty and filial piety, with Us planning in the tent, deciding victory a thousand li away.'
translations.idiomatic: The appointment edict read: 'Thus commands the throne to General of the Van Deng Yu: You have held fast to loyalty and filial devotion, counseled Us within the curtain, and won victories a thousand li away.'

sentences.zh: 孔子曰：「自吾有回，門人日親。

translations.literal: Confucius said: "Since I had Hui, the disciples daily drew closer."'
translations.idiomatic: Confucius said, "Since I gained Yan Hui, my disciples drew daily closer to me."'

sentences.zh: 」斬將破軍，平定山西，功效尤著。

translations.literal: "Beheading generals and breaking armies, pacifying the area west of the mountains, merit and effect were especially marked.'
translations.idiomatic: You have cut down enemy generals, shattered their hosts, and pacified the territory west of the Taihang range; your achievements stand out above the rest.'

sentences.zh: 百姓不親，五品不訓，汝作司徒，敬敷五教，五教在寬。

translations.literal: The hundred surnames are not close; the five grades are not instructed—you shall be minister of education, respectfully spread the five teachings; the five teachings lie in leniency.'
translations.idiomatic: When the people lack harmony and the five relationships lack proper instruction, it is the minister of education who sets them right. Spread the five moral teachings with care, and let those teachings be tempered by leniency.'

sentences.zh: 今遣奉車都尉授印綬，封為酂侯，食邑萬戶。

translations.literal: Now We send the chief charioteer to confer the seal and ribbon, enfeoffing you as marquis of Zan, with food income for ten thousand households.
translations.idiomatic: We therefore send the chief charioteer to present your seal and ribbon and enfeoff you as marquis of Zan with revenue from ten thousand households.

sentences.zh: 敬之哉！

translations.literal: Respect this!'
translations.idiomatic: Bear this charge with reverence!'

sentences.zh: 』禹時年二十四。

translations.literal: Yu at that time was twenty-four years old.
translations.idiomatic: Deng Yu was twenty-four at the time.

sentences.zh: 遂渡汾陰河，入夏陽。

translations.literal: Thereupon he crossed the Fenyin River and entered Xiayang.
translations.idiomatic: He then crossed the Fenyin ford and marched into Xiayang.

sentences.zh: 更始中郎將左輔都尉公乘歙，引其眾十萬，與左馮翊兵共拒禹於衙，禹復破走之，而赤眉遂入長安。

translations.literal: Gengshi's general of the Gentlemen of the Household and colonel of the Left Assistant Regions Gongshe Xi led his host of a hundred thousand and together with the troops of Left Fengyi resisted Yu at Ya; Yu again defeated and drove them off, while the Red Eyebrows thereupon entered Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: Gengshi's household gentleman general and colonel of the left assistant regions, Gongshe Xi, brought a hundred thousand men to join the Left Fengyi garrison in blocking Deng Yu at Ya. Deng Yu routed them again, while the Red Eyebrows pushed on into Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 是時三輔連覆敗，赤眉所過殘賊，百姓不知所歸。

translations.literal: At that time the Three Adjuncts had suffered repeated defeats; wherever the Red Eyebrows passed they ravaged and plundered; the hundred surnames did not know to whom to turn.
translations.idiomatic: The Three Adjuncts had been beaten again and again. The Red Eyebrows left a trail of destruction, and the people had nowhere to look for safety.

sentences.zh: 聞禹乘勝獨克而師行有紀，皆望風相攜負以迎軍，降者日以千數，眾號百萬。

translations.literal: Hearing that Yu alone had won successive victories and that his army's march was disciplined, all looked to the wind, supporting one another on their backs to welcome the army; those who surrendered daily numbered in the thousands, the host was styled a million.
translations.idiomatic: Word spread that Deng Yu was winning every fight and that his troops marched under strict discipline. Families shouldered their children and flocked to meet his columns; thousands surrendered each day until his army was said to number a million.

sentences.zh: 禹所止輒停車住節，以勞來之，父老童稚，重發戴白，滿其車下，莫不感悅，於是名震關西。

translations.literal: Wherever Yu halted he always stopped the chariot and stayed the credentials, thereby to console and summon them; old men and children, carrying burdens on their heads, white-haired, filled beneath his chariot—none were not moved and pleased, and thereupon his name shook Guanxi.
translations.idiomatic: Whenever he halted, he would rein in his carriage and display his credentials to welcome and reassure the people. Gray-haired elders and small children crowded beneath his wheels, and none went away unmoved. His name resounded through the western passes.

sentences.zh: 帝嘉之，數賜書褒美。

translations.literal: The emperor praised him and repeatedly sent letters granting commendation.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor commended him again and again in letters of praise.

sentences.zh: 諸將豪傑皆勸禹徑攻長安。

translations.literal: The generals and bold men all urged Yu to go straight to attack Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: His officers and local leaders all pressed him to strike Chang'an at once.

sentences.zh: 禹曰：『不然。

translations.literal: Yu said: 'It is not so.'
translations.idiomatic: Deng Yu said, 'That would be a mistake.'

sentences.zh: 』今吾眾雖多，能戰者少，前無可仰之積，後無轉饋之資。

translations.literal: Though our host is many now, those who can fight are few; before there is no store to rely on, behind there is no supply of transported provisions.
translations.idiomatic: We may have great numbers, but few of them are real fighters. We have no stockpiled supplies ahead of us and no supply lines behind us.

sentences.zh: 赤眉新拔長安，財富充實，鋒銳未可當也。

translations.literal: The Red Eyebrows have newly taken Chang'an; wealth and supplies are full, their edge is keen and cannot be withstood.
translations.idiomatic: The Red Eyebrows have just seized Chang'an. They sit on full treasuries and their morale is razor-sharp; this is no moment to meet them head-on.

sentences.zh: 夫盜賊群居，無終日之計，財谷雖多，變故萬端，寧能堅守者也？

translations.literal: Now bandits dwell in a mob; they have no plan for the long term; though wealth and grain are much, changes and shifts are of ten thousand kinds—how can they be ones who can hold firm?'
translations.idiomatic: They are nothing but a horde of bandits with no long-term strategy. However rich their grain and loot, they will splinter under a thousand shifting fortunes. How could they possibly hold what they have taken?'

sentences.zh: 上郡、北地、安定三郡，土廣人稀，饒谷多畜，吾且休兵北道，就糧養士，以觀其弊，乃可圖也。

translations.literal: The three commanderies of Shang, Beidi, and Anding have broad land and sparse people, abundant grain and many livestock; I shall for the moment rest the troops on the northern road, draw grain and nurture the soldiers, to observe their decay, and then it can be planned.
translations.idiomatic: Shang, Beidi, and Anding are thinly peopled but rich in grain and herds. I mean to halt the army on the northern road, forage there, rest and feed the men, and watch the rebels wear themselves out. Then we can strike.

sentences.zh: 』於是引軍北至栒邑。

translations.literal: Thereupon he led the army north to Xunyi.
translations.idiomatic: He therefore marched north to Xunyi.

sentences.zh: 禹所到，擊破赤眉別將諸營保，郡邑皆開門歸附。

translations.literal: Wherever Yu arrived he attacked and defeated the separate camps and fortifications of Red Eyebrow subordinate generals; commanderies and cities all opened their gates and submitted.
translations.idiomatic: Wherever he went he overran the Red Eyebrows' detached camps and stockades, and city after city opened its gates to him.

sentences.zh: 西河太守宗育遣子奉檄降，禹遣詣京師。

translations.literal: The grand administrator of Xihe Zong Yu sent his son bearing a written submission to surrender; Yu sent him to proceed to the capital.
translations.idiomatic: Zong Yu, the grand administrator of Xihe, sent his son with a formal surrender; Deng Yu forwarded him to the capital.

sentences.zh: 帝以關中未定，而禹久不進兵，下敕曰：『司徒，堯也；

translations.literal: Because Guanzhong was not yet settled and Yu long did not advance troops, the emperor issued an edict, saying: 'The minister of education is Yao;'
translations.idiomatic: Guanzhong was still unsettled, and Deng Yu had not moved his army for a long time. The emperor sent down an edict: 'My minister of education is a Yao among men;'

sentences.zh: 亡賊，桀也。

translations.literal: the vanished bandits are Jie.
translations.idiomatic: these defeated rebels are no better than Jie.

sentences.zh: 長安吏人，遑遑無所依歸。

translations.literal: The officials and people of Chang'an wander anxiously with nowhere to depend and return.
translations.idiomatic: The officials and commoners of Chang'an wander in terror with nowhere to turn.

sentences.zh: 宜以時進討，鎮慰西京，系百姓之心。

translations.literal: You should in due season advance and punish, pacify and comfort the western capital, and tie the hearts of the hundred surnames.
translations.idiomatic: You should attack at the proper moment, restore calm to the western capital, and win back the people's trust.

sentences.zh: 』禹猶執前意，乃分遣將軍別攻上郡諸縣，更征兵引谷，歸至大要。

translations.literal: Yu still held to his earlier idea, thereupon separately sent generals to attack the counties of Shang commandery, again levied troops and drew grain, returning to Dayao.
translations.idiomatic: Deng Yu stuck to his original plan. He detached generals to raid the counties of Shang, raised more troops, gathered grain, and fell back to Dayao.

sentences.zh: 遣馮愔、宗歆守栒邑。

translations.literal: He sent Feng Yin and Zong Xin to hold Xunyi.
translations.idiomatic: He left Feng Yin and Zong Xin to hold Xunyi.

sentences.zh: 二人爭權相攻，愔遂殺歆，因反擊禹，禹遣使以聞。

translations.literal: The two quarreled over power and attacked each other; Yin thereupon killed Xin, and in turn struck back at Yu; Yu sent an envoy to report it.
translations.idiomatic: The two quarreled over authority and came to blows. Feng Yin killed Zong Xin, then turned on Deng Yu. Deng Yu sent a courier to inform the throne.

sentences.zh: 帝問使人：『愔所親愛為誰？

translations.literal: The emperor asked the envoy: 'Whom does Yin love and favor?'
translations.idiomatic: The emperor asked the messenger, 'Who is Feng Yin's closest confidant?'

sentences.zh: 』對曰：『護軍黃防。

translations.literal: He replied: 'Adjutant Huang Fang.'
translations.idiomatic: The answer came: 'The adjutant Huang Fang.'

sentences.zh: 』帝度愔、防不能久和，勢必相忤，因報禹曰：『縛馮愔者，必黃防也。

translations.literal: The emperor calculated that Yin and Fang could not long be harmonious, the situation would surely lead to mutual injury, and thereupon replied to Yu, saying: 'The one who binds Feng Yin will surely be Huang Fang.'
translations.idiomatic: The emperor reasoned that Feng Yin and Huang Fang could not long remain at peace and would soon fall out. He wrote back to Deng Yu: 'When Feng Yin is taken, it will be Huang Fang who does it.'

sentences.zh: 』乃遣尚書宗廣持節降之。

translations.literal: Thereupon he sent Minister Zong Guang bearing credentials to summon their surrender.
translations.idiomatic: He then sent Minister Zong Guang with imperial credentials to negotiate their submission.

sentences.zh: 後月餘，防果執愔，將其眾歸罪。

translations.literal: After more than a month Fang indeed seized Yin, led his host, and returned to answer for the crime.
translations.idiomatic: A little over a month later Huang Fang seized Feng Yin, brought his troops in, and surrendered for judgment.

sentences.zh: 更始諸將王匡、胡殷等皆詣廣降，與共東歸。

translations.literal: Gengshi's generals Wang Kuang, Hu Yin, and others all went to Guang to surrender, and together with him returned east.
translations.idiomatic: Gengshi's old generals Wang Kuang, Hu Yin, and others presented themselves to Zong Guang and marched east with him.

sentences.zh: 至安邑，道欲亡，廣悉斬之。

translations.literal: Reaching Anyi, on the road they wished to flee; Guang all beheaded them.
translations.idiomatic: When they reached Anyi and tried to slip away en route, Zong Guang executed them all.

sentences.zh: 愔至洛陽，赦不誅。

translations.literal: When Yin reached Luoyang, he was pardoned and not executed.
translations.idiomatic: Feng Yin was pardoned when he arrived in Luoyang and was not put to death.

sentences.zh: 二年春，遣使者更封禹為梁侯，食四縣。

translations.literal: In the spring of the second year an envoy was sent to re-enfeoff Yu as marquis of Liang, with food income for four counties.
translations.idiomatic: In the spring of the second year the court dispatched an envoy to transfer Deng Yu's fief to the marquisate of Liang, with revenue from four counties.

sentences.zh: 時，赤眉西走扶風，禹乃南至長安，軍昆明池，大饗士卒。

translations.literal: At that time the Red Eyebrows went west to Fufeng; Yu thereupon went south to Chang'an, encamped at Kunming Pond, and greatly feasted the soldiers.
translations.idiomatic: The Red Eyebrows had meanwhile withdrawn west into Fufeng. Deng Yu marched south to Chang'an, camped his army at Kunming Pond, and held a grand feast for the troops.

sentences.zh: 率諸將齋戒，擇吉日，修禮謁祠高廟，收十一帝神主，遣使奉詣洛陽，因循行園陵，為置吏士奉守焉。

translations.literal: He led the generals in fasting and purification, chose an auspicious day, performed the rites and visited the shrine of the High Ancestor, collected the spirit tablets of the eleven emperors, sent envoys to escort them to Luoyang, and thereupon toured the imperial parks and tombs, for them setting clerks and soldiers to offer and guard.
translations.idiomatic: He took his officers through the purification rites, picked an auspicious day, and performed sacrifice at Gaozu's temple. He gathered the spirit tablets of eleven Han emperors and sent them under escort to Luoyang, then inspected the imperial tombs and parks and posted men to maintain them.

sentences.zh: 禹引兵與延岑戰於藍田，不克，復就谷雲陽。

translations.literal: Yu led troops and fought Yan Cen at Lantian; he could not overcome him, again went to draw grain at Yunyang.
translations.idiomatic: He offered battle to Yan Cen at Lantian but could not break him, then withdrew to forage in Yunyang.

sentences.zh: 漢中王劉嘉詣禹降。

translations.literal: The king of Hanzhong Liu Jia went to Yu to surrender.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Jia, king of Hanzhong, came to Deng Yu and surrendered.

sentences.zh: 嘉相李寶倨慢無禮，禹斬之。

translations.literal: Jia's chancellor Li Bao was arrogant and rude without propriety; Yu beheaded him.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Jia's chancellor Li Bao treated him with arrogant disrespect; Deng Yu had him executed.

sentences.zh: 寶弟收寶部曲擊禹，殺將軍耿訢。

translations.literal: Bao's younger brother gathered Bao's retainers and struck Yu, killing general Geng Xin.
translations.idiomatic: Li Bao's younger brother rallied his brother's followers, attacked Deng Yu, and killed General Geng Xin.

sentences.zh: 自馮愔反後，禹威稍損，又乏食，歸附者離散。

translations.literal: From the time of Feng Yin's rebellion Yu's prestige gradually diminished, and again lacking food, those who had submitted scattered.
translations.idiomatic: After Feng Yin's mutiny Deng Yu's prestige ebbed, and with supplies running short his adherents began to melt away.

sentences.zh: 而赤眉復還入長安，禹與戰，敗走，至高陵，軍士饑餓，皆食棗菜。

translations.literal: And the Red Eyebrows again returned and entered Chang'an; Yu fought them, was defeated and fled, reaching Gaoling; the soldiers were hungry and all ate jujubes and wild greens.
translations.idiomatic: The Red Eyebrows swept back into Chang'an. Deng Yu gave battle, was routed, and fell back to Gaoling. His men were starving and lived on jujubes and weeds.

sentences.zh: 帝乃征禹還，敕曰：『赤眉無谷，自當來東，吾折捶笞之，非諸將憂也。

translations.literal: The emperor thereupon summoned Yu back, with an edict saying: 'The Red Eyebrows lack grain; they themselves will come east; I shall break the whip and flog them—it is not the generals' worry.'
translations.idiomatic: The emperor recalled Deng Yu with orders: 'The Red Eyebrows are out of grain; they will drift east on their own. I will break them like kindling. This is no longer the generals' concern.'

sentences.zh: 無得復妄進兵。

translations.literal: Do not again rashly advance troops.'
translations.idiomatic: Do not thrust your army forward without cause.'

sentences.zh: 』禹慚於受任而功不遂，數以饑卒僥戰，輒不利。

translations.literal: Yu was ashamed at having received a commission yet merit not being accomplished, repeatedly with hungry soldiers hazardously fought battles, always faring badly.
translations.idiomatic: Deng Yu was mortified that such a great charge had come to so little. Again and again he threw famished troops into risky fights and lost every time.

sentences.zh: 三年春，與車騎將軍鄧弘擊赤眉，遂為所敗，眾皆死散。

translations.literal: In the spring of the third year, together with the general of chariots and cavalry Deng Hong he struck the Red Eyebrows and was thereupon defeated by them; the host all died or scattered.
translations.idiomatic: In the spring of the third year he joined Deng Hong the general of chariots and cavalry against the Red Eyebrows and was shattered; his army was killed or scattered to the winds.

sentences.zh: 事在《馮異傳》。

translations.literal: The matter is recorded in the biography of Feng Yi.
translations.idiomatic: The full account appears in Feng Yi's biography.

sentences.zh: 獨與二十四騎還詣宜陽，謝上大司徒、梁侯印綬。

translations.literal: Alone with twenty-four riders he returned to Yiyang, apologizing and presenting up the grand minister of education and marquis of Liang seals and ribbons.
translations.idiomatic: He rode into Yiyang with only twenty-four horsemen, returned the seals of grand minister of education and marquis of Liang, and offered his apology.

sentences.zh: 有詔歸侯印綬。

translations.literal: There was an edict to return the marquis's seal and ribbon.
translations.idiomatic: An edict sent back his marquis's seal and ribbon.

sentences.zh: 數月，拜右將軍。

translations.literal: After several months he was appointed general of the right.
translations.idiomatic: A few months later he was named general of the right.

sentences.zh: 延岑自敗於東陽，遂與秦豐合。

translations.literal: Yan Cen, from having been defeated at Dongyang, thereupon joined with Qin Feng.
translations.idiomatic: After his defeat at Dongyang, Yan Cen threw in his lot with Qin Feng.

sentences.zh: 四年春，復寇順陽間。

translations.literal: In the spring of the fourth year he again raided the region of Shunyang.
translations.idiomatic: In the spring of the fourth year he struck again along the Shunyang frontier.

sentences.zh: 遣禹護復漢將軍鄧曄、輔漢將軍於匡，擊破岑於鄧；

translations.literal: He sent Yu to protect the general who restores Han Deng Ye and the general who assists Han Yu Kuang, who defeated Cen at Deng;
translations.idiomatic: The emperor dispatched Deng Yu to support Deng Ye, general who restores Han, and Yu Kuang, general who assists Han; together they crushed Yan Cen at Deng.

sentences.zh: 追至武當，復破之。

translations.literal: Pursuing to Wudang, again defeated him.
translations.idiomatic: They pursued him to Wudang and broke his army a second time.

sentences.zh: 岑奔漢中，餘黨悉降。

translations.literal: Cen fled to Hanzhong; the remaining band all surrendered.
translations.idiomatic: Yan Cen fled into Hanzhong, while every remnant of his force submitted.

sentences.zh: 十三年，天下平定，諸功臣皆增戶邑，定封禹為高密侯，食高密、昌安、夷安、淳於四縣。

translations.literal: In the thirteenth year the realm was pacified; all meritorious ministers had their household fiefs increased, and Yu was definitively enfeoffed as marquis of Gaomi, with revenue from the four counties of Gaomi, Chang'an, Yi'an, and Chunyu.
translations.idiomatic: In the thirteenth year, with the empire at peace, the court enlarged the fiefs of the founding ministers and fixed Deng Yu's title as marquis of Gaomi, with income from Gaomi, Chang'an, Yi'an, and Chunyu.

sentences.zh: 帝以禹功高，封弟寬為明親侯。

translations.literal: Because Yu's merit was high, the emperor enfeoffed his younger brother Kuan as marquis of Mingqin.
translations.idiomatic: In recognition of Deng Yu's towering service, his brother Deng Kuan was made marquis of Mingqin.

sentences.zh: 其後左右將軍官罷，以特進奉朝請。

translations.literal: After that the offices of generals of the left and right were abolished, and he was given eminent rank and attendance at court on summons.
translations.idiomatic: Later the posts of left and right general were discontinued; Deng Yu received the honorary rank of special advancement and was summoned to court as protocol allowed.

sentences.zh: 禹內文明，篤行淳備，事母至孝。

translations.literal: Yu was inwardly cultured and enlightened, his conduct sincere and complete, serving his mother with utmost filial piety.
translations.idiomatic: Deng Yu was refined and discerning by nature, unfailingly honest in conduct, and devoted to his mother in the extreme.

sentences.zh: 天下既定，常欲遠名勢。

translations.literal: Once the realm was settled, he always wished to keep clear of fame and power.
translations.idiomatic: After peace returned, he habitually sought distance from celebrity and political ambition.

sentences.zh: 有子十三人，各使守一藝。

translations.literal: He had thirteen sons, each of whom he had guard a single craft.
translations.idiomatic: He fathered thirteen sons and apprenticed each to a different skill or specialty.

sentences.zh: 修整閨門，教養子孫，皆可以為後世法。

translations.literal: He ordered the inner quarters, teaching and nurturing sons and grandsons, all of whom could serve as models for later generations.
translations.idiomatic: He kept good order in the household, rearing sons and grandsons by example until they became patterns for posterity.

sentences.zh: 資用國邑，不修產利。

translations.literal: In expenses he used the income of his state; he did not pursue profit from property.
translations.idiomatic: He lived on his fief's revenue and never schemed to enlarge his private estates.

sentences.zh: 帝益重之。

translations.literal: The emperor increasingly esteemed him.
translations.idiomatic: The sovereign's regard for him only deepened.

sentences.zh: 中元元年，復行司徒事。

translations.literal: In the first year of Zhongyuan he again exercised the duties of minister of education.
translations.idiomatic: In Zhongyuan 1 he resumed the portfolio of grand minister of education.

sentences.zh: 從東巡狩，封岱宗。

translations.literal: Following on the eastern tour of inspection, he was enfeoffed at Dai, the Lofty Ancestor of the mountains.
translations.idiomatic: He accompanied the emperor on the eastern progress and received his share of the sacrifice at Mount Tai.

sentences.zh: 顯宗即位，以禹先帝元功，拜為太傅，進見東向，甚見尊寵。

translations.literal: When Emperor Xianzong ascended, because Yu was a founding minister of the late emperor, he was appointed grand tutor, advanced and received facing east, and was greatly honored and favored.
translations.idiomatic: When Emperor Ming ascended the throne, Deng Yu was named grand tutor as a pillar of the former reign; he was granted the eastern-facing seat reserved for the most honored ministers and enjoyed extraordinary favor.

sentences.zh: 居歲餘，寢疾。

translations.literal: After residing a bit more than a year, he lay ill in bed.
translations.idiomatic: A little over a year later he took to his bed with a serious illness.

sentences.zh: 帝數自臨問，以子男二人為郎。

translations.literal: The emperor repeatedly came in person to inquire, appointing two of his sons gentlemen of the palace.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor visited him again and again at his bedside and ennobled two of his sons as gentlemen of the palace.

sentences.zh: 永平元年，年五十七薨，謚曰元侯。

translations.literal: In the first year of Yongping, at age fifty-seven he died; his posthumous title was Marquis Yuan.
translations.idiomatic: He died in Yongping 1 at the age of fifty-seven and received the posthumous epithet Yuan, meaning the foremost among peers.

sentences.zh: 帝分禹封為三國：長子震為高密侯，襲為昌安侯，珍為夷安侯。

translations.literal: The emperor divided Yu's fief into three states: the eldest son Zhen was marquis of Gaomi, Xi marquis of Chang'an, and Zhen marquis of Yi'an.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor split Deng Yu's marquisate three ways: the eldest son became marquis of Gaomi, another son marquis of Chang'an, and a third marquis of Yi'an.

sentences.zh: 禹少子鴻，好籌策。

translations.literal: Yu's youngest son Hong was fond of stratagems.
translations.idiomatic: Deng Yu's youngest son, Deng Hong, had a passion for military planning.

sentences.zh: 永平中，以為小侯。

translations.literal: In the Yongping period he was made a minor marquis.
translations.idiomatic: During the Yongping era he was given a junior marquisate.

sentences.zh: 引入與議邊事，帝以為能，拜將兵長史，率五營士屯雁門。

translations.literal: He was brought in to deliberate on frontier affairs; the emperor considered him capable, appointed him senior clerk to the general commanding troops, and led five-camp soldiers to garrison Yanmen.
translations.idiomatic: Summoned to counsel on the borders, he impressed the court as able; he was named senior clerk to a commanding general and led troops of the five camps to hold Yanmen.

sentences.zh: 肅宗時，為度遼將軍。

translations.literal: In Emperor Suzong's time he was general who crosses the Liao.
translations.idiomatic: Under Emperor Zhang he rose to general who crosses the Liao.

sentences.zh: 永元中，與大將軍竇憲俱出擊匈奴，有功，征行車騎將軍。

translations.literal: In the Yongyuan period, together with the grand general Dou Xian he went out to strike the Xiongnu, had merit, and was promoted acting general of chariots and cavalry.
translations.idiomatic: In the Yongyuan years he campaigned with Grand General Dou Xian against the Xiongnu, distinguished himself, and was advanced to acting general of chariots and cavalry.

sentences.zh: 出塞追畔胡逢侯，坐逗留，下獄死。

translations.literal: Going beyond the passes in pursuit of the rebel Hu Fenghou, for loitering and delay he was cast into prison and died.
translations.idiomatic: He led a column beyond the frontier after the renegade Fenghou but was charged with dilatory command, imprisoned, and died there.

sentences.zh: 高密侯震卒，子乾嗣。

translations.literal: Marquis of Gaomi Zhen died; his son Qian succeeded.
translations.idiomatic: When the marquis of Gaomi died, his son Deng Qian inherited the title.

sentences.zh: 乾尚顯宗女沁水公主。

translations.literal: Qian married the Princess of Qinshui, Emperor Xianzong's daughter.
translations.idiomatic: Deng Qian wed the Princess of Qinshui, a daughter of Emperor Ming.

sentences.zh: 永元十四年，陰皇后巫蠱事發，乾從兄奉以後舅被誅，乾從坐，國除。

translations.literal: In the fourteenth year of Yongyuan the affair of Empress Yin's witchcraft broke out; Qian's elder cousin Feng was executed as the empress's uncle; Qian sat in guilt for association, and the state was abolished.
translations.idiomatic: In Yongyuan 14 the witchcraft case of Empress Yin exploded. Deng Qian's cousin Deng Feng, the empress's uncle, was executed; Deng Qian was implicated, stripped of rank, and his fief extinguished.

sentences.zh: 元興元年，和帝復封乾本國，拜侍中。

translations.literal: In the first year of Yuanxing, Emperor He restored Qian to his original state and appointed him palace attendant.
translations.idiomatic: In Yuanxing 1 Emperor He restored Deng Qian's old marquisate and named him a palace attendant.

sentences.zh: 乾卒，子成嗣。

translations.literal: When Qian died, his son Cheng succeeded.
translations.idiomatic: Deng Qian was succeeded by his son Deng Cheng.

sentences.zh: 成卒，子褒嗣。

translations.literal: When Cheng died, his son Bao succeeded.
translations.idiomatic: Deng Cheng was succeeded in turn by his son Deng Bao.

sentences.zh: 褒尚安帝妹舞陰長公主，桓帝時為少府。

translations.literal: Bao married the Princess of Wuyin, younger sister of Emperor An; in Emperor Huan's time he was minister of the lesser treasury.
translations.idiomatic: Deng Bao married the Princess of Wuyin, Emperor An's younger sister, and under Emperor Huan served as minister of the lesser treasury.

sentences.zh: 褒卒，長子某嗣。

translations.literal: When Bao died, a certain eldest son succeeded.
translations.idiomatic: When Deng Bao died, his eldest son succeeded him; the text leaves that heir unnamed.

sentences.zh: 少子昌襲母爵為舞陰侯，拜黃門侍郎。

translations.literal: The younger son Chang inherited his mother's noble rank as marquis of Wuyin and was appointed gentleman at the yellow gates.
translations.idiomatic: The younger son, Deng Chang, succeeded to his mother's title as marquis of Wuyin and became a gentleman at the yellow gates.

sentences.zh: 昌安侯襲嗣子藩，亦尚顯宗女平臯長公主，和帝時為侍中。

translations.literal: Marquis of Chang'an Xi's successor Fan also married Emperor Xianzong's daughter the Princess of Pinggao, and in Emperor He's time was palace attendant.
translations.idiomatic: Fan, heir to the Chang'an marquisate of Deng Xi, likewise married the Princess of Pinggao, another daughter of Emperor Ming, and served as palace attendant under Emperor He.

sentences.zh: 夷安侯珍子康，少有操行。

translations.literal: Marquis of Yi'an Zhen's son Kang, in youth had conduct and integrity.
translations.idiomatic: Deng Kang, son of the marquis of Yi'an, showed moral fiber even as a young man.

sentences.zh: 兄良襲封，無後，永初六年，紹封康為夷安侯。

translations.literal: His elder brother Liang inherited the fief and had no heir; in the sixth year of Yongchu Kang was again enfeoffed as marquis of Yi'an.
translations.idiomatic: His elder brother Deng Liang had held the title but left no son; in Yongchu 6 Deng Kang was re-enfeoffed as marquis of Yi'an.

sentences.zh: 時諸紹封者皆食故國半租，康以皇太后戚屬，獨三分食二，以侍祠侯為越騎校尉。

translations.literal: At that time all who received renewed fiefs took half the old state's rent; Kang, as a kinsman of the empress dowager, alone took two-thirds, and as marquis who attends sacrifice was colonel of the agile cavalry.
translations.idiomatic: Renewed fiefs normally drew only half the old revenue, but Deng Kang, as the empress dowager's kin, received two-thirds; he held the title of marquis who attends sacrifice and served as colonel of agile cavalry.

sentences.zh: 康以太后久臨朝政，宗門盛滿，數上書長樂宮諫爭，宜崇公室，自損私權，言甚切至。

translations.literal: Kang, because the empress dowager long presided over court government and the clan gate was full to overflowing, repeatedly sent memorials to Changle Palace remonstrating that the imperial house should be exalted and private power curtailed—his words were extremely cutting and thorough.
translations.idiomatic: With the empress dowager ruling from behind the curtain and his clan swollen with influence, Deng Kang repeatedly memorialized the Changle Palace, urging her to elevate the public authority of the Liu house and rein in the Dengs' private power—his language was blunt and uncompromising.

sentences.zh: 太后不從。

translations.literal: The empress dowager did not follow.
translations.idiomatic: She ignored his advice.

sentences.zh: 康心懷畏懼，永寧元年，遂謝病不朝。

translations.literal: Kang, harboring fear, in the first year of Yongning thereupon pleaded illness and did not attend court.
translations.idiomatic: Frightened for his safety, in Yongning 1 Deng Kang feigned illness and stayed away from the court.

sentences.zh: 太后使內侍者問之。

translations.literal: The empress dowager sent inner attendants to inquire of him.
translations.idiomatic: The empress dowager dispatched palace attendants to question him.

sentences.zh: 時宮人出入，多能有所毀譽，其中耆宿皆稱中大人。

translations.literal: At that time palace women coming and going mostly could bring slander or praise; among them the aged veterans were all styled great persons within.
translations.idiomatic: Court ladies who passed in and out could make or ruin reputations at whim; the senior eunuchs among them were reverently addressed as inner-court magnates.

sentences.zh: 所使者乃康家先婢，亦自通中大人。

translations.literal: The one sent was originally a maid of Kang's household, who also styled herself a great person within.
translations.idiomatic: The envoy turned out to be a former bondmaid from Deng Kang's own household, who now claimed the same inner-court title.

sentences.zh: 康聞，詬之曰：『汝我家出，亦敢爾耶？

translations.literal: Kang heard and reviled her, saying: 'You came out of my house—how dare you behave thus?'
translations.idiomatic: Deng Kang heard of it and berated her: 'You were a servant in my family—how dare you put on such airs?'

sentences.zh: 』婢怨恚，還說康詐疾而言不遜。

translations.literal: The maid nursed resentment, returned and reported that Kang feigned illness and spoke disrespectfully.
translations.idiomatic: Smarting from the insult, she went back and told the palace that Deng Kang was shamming illness and had spoken lese-majeste.

sentences.zh: 太后大怒，遂免康官，遣歸國，絕屬籍。

translations.literal: The empress dowager was greatly angered, thereupon removed Kang from office, sent him back to his state, and struck him from the imperial genealogy.
translations.idiomatic: The empress dowager was furious: she dismissed Deng Kang from office, banished him to his fief, and expunged his name from the rolls of the imperial clan.

sentences.zh: 及從兄騭誅，安帝征康為侍中。

translations.literal: When his elder cousin Zhi was executed, Emperor An summoned Kang as palace attendant.
translations.idiomatic: After his cousin Deng Zhi was put to death, Emperor An recalled Deng Kang as palace attendant.

sentences.zh: 順帝立，為太僕，有方正稱，名重朝廷。

translations.literal: When Emperor Shun was established, he was grand coach of the heir apparent, had a reputation for integrity, and his name weighed heavily at court.
translations.idiomatic: Under Emperor Shun he became grand coach of the heir apparent, famed for upright character and commanding great respect at court.

sentences.zh: 以病免，加位特進。

translations.literal: He was dismissed for illness and given in addition the rank of special advancement.
translations.idiomatic: Illness forced his retirement, but the court added the honorific rank of special advancement.

sentences.zh: 陽嘉三年卒，謚曰義侯。

translations.literal: He died in the third year of Yangjia; his posthumous title was Marquis Yi.
translations.idiomatic: He died in Yangjia 3 with the posthumous name Yi, the righteous.

sentences.zh: 論曰：『夫變通之世，君臣相擇，斯最作事謀始之幾也。

translations.literal: The discussion says: 'In a generation of change and adaptation, ruler and minister choose each other—this is the decisive hinge on which every undertaking turns at its inception.'
translations.idiomatic: The historian's judgment runs: 'When times shift and institutions bend, sovereign and servant select one another—that first choice is the pivot on which every later success or failure turns.'

sentences.zh: 鄧公嬴糧徒步，觸紛亂而赴光武，可謂識所從會矣。

translations.literal: Lord Deng shouldered grain and went on foot, plunged through chaos to join Guangwu—one may say he knew whom to follow and seized the moment of convergence.
translations.idiomatic: Deng Yu packed his own provisions and walked to war, cutting through anarchy to reach Guangwu—he knew where destiny lay and threw in his lot at the right instant.

sentences.zh: 於是中分麾下之軍，以臨山西之隙，至使關河響動，懷赴如歸。

translations.literal: Thereupon he split the army under his command in half to exploit the opening west of the mountains, until the passes and rivers trembled with the sound and men rushed to join him as if returning home.
translations.idiomatic: He then detached half his command to strike through the breach west of the Taihang range until the Yellow River and the passes echoed with his advance and recruits poured in as though coming home.

sentences.zh: 功雖不遂，而道亦弘矣！

translations.literal: Though merit was not finally achieved, the way he took was grand nonetheless!
translations.idiomatic: He never quite finished the conquest, yet the path he opened was magnificent in its own right!

sentences.zh: 及其威損栒邑，兵散宜陽，褫龍章於終朝，就侯服以卒歲，榮悴交而下無二色，進退用而上無猜情，使君臣之美，後世莫窺其間，不亦君子之致為乎！

translations.literal: Coming to when his might was broken at Xunyi and his soldiers scattered at Yiyang, when in a single morning the dragon insignia were stripped away and he donned a marquis's robes to live out his years—glory and ruin alternated yet he showed no second color below; advance and retreat were each put to use yet above there was no suspicion—such was the beauty of ruler and minister that later ages cannot peer into the seam between them: is this not the consummation of the gentleman's conduct!
translations.idiomatic: Later, when his prestige crumbled at Xunyi and his army melted away at Yiyang, when imperial favor was withdrawn in a morning yet he accepted a marquis's quiet retirement—honor and disgrace chased each other while he betrayed not a flicker of resentment; he was used and set aside while the throne never quite lost trust in him. That seamless grace between lord and servant is a closeness later ages can scarcely glimpse: is it not the perfection of what a gentleman may become!

sentences.zh: 訓字平叔，禹第六子也。

translations.literal: Xun, courtesy name Pingshu, was Yu's sixth son.
translations.idiomatic: Deng Xun, whose courtesy name was Pingshu, was the sixth son of Deng Yu.

sentences.zh: 少有大志，不好文學，禹常非之。

translations.literal: In youth he had great ambitions and did not love literary studies; Yu often criticized him.
translations.idiomatic: As a boy he aimed high and disdained bookish pursuits, to his father's frequent disapproval.

sentences.zh: 顯宗即位，初以為郎中。

translations.literal: When Emperor Xianzong first ascended, he was first appointed gentleman of the palace.
translations.idiomatic: When Emperor Ming first took the throne, Deng Xun began his career as a gentleman of the palace.

sentences.zh: 訓樂施下士，士大夫多歸之。

translations.literal: Xun delighted in generosity toward men of lower rank; many gentlemen and great officers attached themselves to him.
translations.idiomatic: Deng Xun loved to spend his wealth on humble talents, and a host of scholars and officials gathered around him.

sentences.zh: 永平中，理虖沱、石臼河，從都慮至羊腸倉，欲令通漕。

translations.literal: In the Yongping period he regulated the Hutuo and Shijiu rivers, from Dulü to Yangchang Granary, wishing to open grain transport by water.
translations.idiomatic: During Yongping he supervised work on the Hutuo and Shijiu waterways from Dulü to Yangchang Granary, hoping to carve out a canal for tribute grain.

sentences.zh: 太原吏人苦役，連年無成，轉運所經三百八十九隘，前後沒溺死者不可勝算。

translations.literal: The clerks and people of Taiyuan suffered under the corvée; year after year nothing was completed; along the three hundred eighty-nine narrows the transport passed, those who drowned before and after were beyond calculation.
translations.idiomatic: The people of Taiyuan groaned under endless labor gangs; the channel never advanced, and along its three hundred eighty-nine choke points the drowned—before and after—were countless.

sentences.zh: 建初三年，拜訓謁者，使監領其事。

translations.literal: In the third year of Jianchu he appointed Xun usher and had him supervise and direct the affair.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianchu 3 the court named Deng Xun an usher and put him in charge of the project.

sentences.zh: 訓考量隱括，知大功難立，具以上言。

translations.literal: Xun measured and investigated, knew a great achievement was hard to establish, and set forth the whole matter in a memorial.
translations.idiomatic: Deng Xun surveyed the ground, saw that the grand design could not succeed, and laid the facts before the throne in a full report.

sentences.zh: 肅宗從之，遂罷其役，更用驢輦，歲省費億萬計，全活徒士數千人。

translations.literal: Emperor Suzong followed his advice, thereupon stopped the labor, instead used donkey carts, yearly saving expenses reckoned in the hundreds of millions, and wholly preserved several thousand conscript laborers alive.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Zhang accepted his counsel, canceled the dig, switched to mule-drawn transport, saved untold millions each year, and spared thousands of corvée workers' lives.

sentences.zh: 會上谷太守任興欲誅赤沙烏桓，烏桓怨恨謀反，詔訓將黎陽營兵屯狐奴，以防其變。

translations.literal: It happened that the grand administrator of Shanggu, Ren Xing, wished to execute the Red Sands Wuhuan; the Wuhuan nursed hatred and plotted rebellion; an edict ordered Xun to lead Liyang camp troops and garrison Hunu to guard against the crisis.
translations.idiomatic: About then Shanggu Administrator Ren Xing planned a purge of the Red Sands Wuhuan; they plotted revenge. Deng Xun was ordered to take the Liyang garrison and hold Hunu against an outbreak.

sentences.zh: 訓撫接邊民，為幽部所歸。

translations.literal: Xun comforted and received the frontier people and was trusted by You province.
translations.idiomatic: He won the border population by kindness and became the man Youzhou looked to.

sentences.zh: 六年，遷護烏桓校尉，黎陽故人多攜將老幼，樂隨訓徙邊。

translations.literal: In the sixth year he was transferred to colonel protecting the Wuhuan; many old friends from Liyang carried off the aged and young, happy to follow Xun to the frontier.
translations.idiomatic: In the sixth year he was promoted colonel for the protection of the Wuhuan; old comrades from Liyang packed up families and gladly followed him to the border.

sentences.zh: 鮮卑聞其威恩，皆不敢南近塞下。

translations.literal: The Xianbei heard of his awe and kindness and none dared approach south of the passes.
translations.idiomatic: The Xianbei, hearing of his blend of terror and mercy, would not venture south of the garrisons.

sentences.zh: 八年，舞陰公主子梁扈有罪，訓坐私與扈通書，征免歸閭里。

translations.literal: In the eighth year the Princess of Wuyin's son Liang Hu had a crime; Xun sat in guilt for privately exchanging letters with Hu, was summoned, dismissed, and returned to his village.
translations.idiomatic: In the eighth year Liang Hu, son of the Princess of Wuyin, was charged with a crime; Deng Xun was implicated for private correspondence with him, recalled, stripped of office, and sent home.

sentences.zh: 元和三年，盧水胡反畔，以訓為竭者，乘傳到武威，拜張掖太守。

translations.literal: In the third year of Yuanhe the Lu River Hu rebelled; Xun was made usher, rode post to Wuwei, and was appointed grand administrator of Zhangye.
translations.idiomatic: In Yuanhe 3 the Lu River Hu rose in revolt; Deng Xun was named court usher, rode relay to Wuwei, and took office as grand administrator of Zhangye.

sentences.zh: 章和二年，護羌校尉張紆繡誅燒當種羌迷吾等，由是諸羌大怒，謀欲報怨，朝廷憂之。

translations.literal: In the second year of Zhanghe, the colonel protecting the Qiang Zhang Yu executed Mixian and others of the Burn-Dang Qiang, whereupon the various Qiang were greatly angered and plotted revenge; the court worried.
translations.idiomatic: In Zhanghe 2 Zhang Yu, colonel for the protection of the Qiang, executed Mixian and other leaders of the Burn-Dang tribe; the Qiang confederations exploded in fury and swore vengeance, and the capital trembled.

sentences.zh: 公卿舉訓代紆為校尉。

translations.literal: The dukes and ministers recommended Xun to replace Yu as colonel.
translations.idiomatic: The high ministers nominated Deng Xun to succeed Zhang Yu as protector colonel.

sentences.zh: 諸羌激忿，遂相與解仇結婚，交質盟詛，眾四萬餘人，期冰合渡河攻訓。

translations.literal: The various Qiang, fiercely resentful, thereupon together undid old feuds, intermarried, exchanged hostages, swore oaths, and mustered more than forty thousand men, planning when the ice closed to cross the river and attack Xun.
translations.idiomatic: The Qiang clans, white-hot with rage, buried old quarrels, intermarried, exchanged hostages, swore blood oaths, and raised over forty thousand warriors to ford the Yellow River on winter ice and fall on Deng Xun.

sentences.zh: 先是，小月氏胡分居塞內，勝兵者二三千騎，緣勇健富強，每與羌戰，常以少制多。

translations.literal: Earlier, the Lesser Yuezhi Hu had dwelt separately inside the passes; those fit for battle were two or three thousand horse; relying on bravery, vigor, and wealth, whenever they fought the Qiang they often used few to control many.
translations.idiomatic: The Lesser Yuezhi tribes had long lived inside the frontier; they could field two or three thousand good riders and, being fierce and well-equipped, habitually beat larger Qiang forces with handfuls of men.

sentences.zh: 雖首施兩端，漢亦時收其用。

translations.literal: Though they swayed between two masters, the Han also at times collected use from them.
translations.idiomatic: Though they hedged between Han and barbarian, the court still found them useful on occasion.

sentences.zh: 時迷吾子迷唐，別與武威種羌合兵萬騎，來至塞下，未敢攻訓，先欲脅月氏胡，訓擁衛稽故，令不得戰。

translations.literal: At that time Mixian's son Mitang separately joined with Wuhuan Qiang of Wuwei in ten thousand horse, came to below the passes, did not dare attack Xun, first wished to coerce the Yuezhi Hu; Xun shielded and screened Jigu, ordering that they could not fight.
translations.idiomatic: Mitang, son of Mixian, then combined ten thousand Wuhuan and Qiang riders from Wuwei and rode to the frontier. Unwilling to strike Deng Xun head-on, he tried first to bully the Lesser Yuezhi; Deng Xun threw a protective cordon around their chief Jigu and forbade them to give battle.

sentences.zh: 議者，咸以羌胡相攻，縣官之利，以夷伐夷，不宜禁護。

translations.literal: Those who deliberated all thought that Qiang and Hu attacking each other was profit to the county official—using barbarians to strike barbarians—and that one should not prohibit and protect.
translations.idiomatic: Advisers argued that pitting Qiang against Hu served the empire—let barbarian fight barbarian—and that Deng Xun ought not shield the Yuezhi.

sentences.zh: 訓曰：『不然。

translations.literal: Xun said: 'It is not so.'
translations.idiomatic: Deng Xun replied, 'That is shortsighted.'

sentences.zh: 今張紆失信，眾羌大動，經常屯兵，不下二萬，轉運之費，空竭府帑，涼州吏人，命縣絲發。

translations.literal: Now Zhang Yu has lost trust; the host of Qiang is greatly stirred; standing troops regularly garrisoned are not less than twenty thousand; the cost of transport empties the treasury; the clerks and people of Liangzhou hang by a thread for their lives.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Yu has forfeited the tribes' trust and set every Qiang clan in motion. We keep twenty thousand men under arms out west and bleed the treasury dry supplying them. The people of Liangzhou hang by a thread.

sentences.zh: 原諸胡所以難得意者，皆恩信不厚耳。

translations.literal: Tracing why the various Hu are hard to win to satisfaction, it is all that favor and trust are not thick.
translations.idiomatic: If you ask why the frontier tribes never quite submit, the answer is always that Han good faith has been too thin.

sentences.zh: 今因其迫急，以德懷之，庶能有用。

translations.literal: Now because they are pressed and urgent, if with virtue we cherish them, perhaps they can be of use.
translations.idiomatic: They are desperate; win them with a steady show of kindness now and they may yet fight for us.

sentences.zh: 』遂令開城及所居園門，悉驅群胡妻子內之，嚴兵守衛。

translations.literal: Thereupon he ordered the city gates and the garden gates where they dwelt opened, drove in all the wives and children of the Hu bands, and strictly guarded them with troops.
translations.idiomatic: He threw open the city gates and the compound gates, herded every Hu wife and child inside under arms, and sealed the walls behind them.

sentences.zh: 羌掠無所得，又不敢逼諸胡，因即解去。

translations.literal: The Qiang plundered and obtained nothing, again did not dare press the various Hu, and thereupon broke up and departed.
translations.idiomatic: The Qiang raiders found nothing to seize, dared not storm Deng Xun's Hu wards, and broke off the siege.

sentences.zh: 由是湟中諸胡皆言『漢家常欲鬥我曹，今鄧使君待我以恩信，開門內我妻子，乃得父母。

translations.literal: Thereupon the various Hu of the middle Huang said: 'The Han house always wished to make us fight one another; now Director Deng treats us with favor and trust, opens the gates and takes in our wives and children, so that we obtain father and mother.'
translations.idiomatic: The Huang-shore Hu cried, 'The Han court has always tried to set us at each other's throats. Now Director Deng deals in good faith: he opens the gates, shelters our wives and children, and gives us back our families.'

sentences.zh: 』咸歡喜叩頭曰：『唯使君所命。

translations.literal: All rejoiced, knocked their heads, and said: 'We obey whatever the director commands.'
translations.idiomatic: They kowtowed for joy and swore, 'We will obey Director Deng in everything.'

sentences.zh: 』訓遂撫養其中少年勇者數百人，以為義從。

translations.literal: Xun thereupon nurtured and raised several hundred of the brave youths among them, making them righteous followers.
translations.idiomatic: Deng Xun then adopted several hundred of the boldest young men from among them as sworn retainers.

sentences.zh: 羌胡俗恥病死，每病臨困，輒以刃自刺。

translations.literal: By Qiang and Hu custom they are ashamed to die of illness; whenever illness reaches extremity, they always stab themselves with a blade.
translations.idiomatic: Qiang and Hu alike reckon death by sickness a disgrace; the dying often fall on their knives.

sentences.zh: 訓聞有困疾者，輒拘持縛束，不與兵刃，使醫藥療之，愈者非一，小大莫不感悅。

translations.literal: Whenever Xun heard of one who was gravely ill, he always seized and bound him, did not give him a weapon, had him treated with medicine and drugs, and those cured were not one only; great and small none were not moved and pleased.
translations.idiomatic: Whenever Deng Xun heard that a tribesman lay desperately ill, he had the man seized, bound, and disarmed, then nursed him with physicians and drugs. Many lived; the whole frontier was moved.

sentences.zh: 於是賞賂諸羌種，使相招誘。

translations.literal: Thereupon he used gifts to reward the various Qiang tribes, making them entice one another.
translations.idiomatic: He then distributed bribes among the Qiang bands and set them to lure each other in.

sentences.zh: 迷唐伯父號吾乃將其母及種人八百戶，自塞外來降。

translations.literal: Mitang's uncle Haowu thereupon led his mother and eight hundred households of his tribe, coming from beyond the passes to surrender.
translations.idiomatic: Mitang's uncle Haowu then shepherded eight hundred households, with the women and elders of the line, in from beyond the frontier to defect.

sentences.zh: 訓因發湟中秦、胡、羌兵四千人出塞，掩擊迷唐於寫谷，斬首虜六百餘人，得馬、牛、羊萬餘頭。

translations.literal: Xun thereupon mobilized four thousand Qin, Hu, and Qiang troops of the middle Huang, went beyond the passes, and fell on Mitang at Xie Valley, beheading over six hundred captives and taking more than ten thousand head of horses, cattle, and sheep.
translations.idiomatic: Deng Xun then marched four thousand Huang-shore Han, Hu, and Qiang troops beyond the wall and ambushed Mitang in Xie Gorge, taking six hundred heads and over ten thousand head of livestock.

sentences.zh: 迷唐乃去大、小榆，居頗巖谷，眾悉破散。

translations.literal: Mitang thereupon left Great and Lesser Yu and dwelt in the precipitous Yan valley; the host all broke and scattered.
translations.idiomatic: Mitang abandoned the Great and Lesser Yu strongholds for the rugged Yan gorges; his following disintegrated.

sentences.zh: 其春，復欲歸故地就田業，訓乃發湟中六千人，令長史任尚將之，縫革為船，置於箄上以度河，掩擊迷唐廬落大豪，多所斬獲。

translations.literal: That spring he again wished to return to the old lands and take up farming; Xun thereupon sent six thousand men of the middle Huang, ordered Senior Clerk Ren Shang to lead them, sewed hides into boats, placed them on raft frames to cross the river, and fell on Mitang's encampments and great chiefs, with many beheadings and captures.
translations.idiomatic: Next spring Mitang tried to slip back to his old pastures. Deng Xun sent six thousand Huang-shore troops under Ren Shang, who stitched hides into boats, floated them on bamboo rafts across the river, and stormed Mitang's camps, cutting down many chiefs.

sentences.zh: 復追逐奔北，會尚等夜為羌所攻，於是義從羌胡並力破之，斬首前後一千八百餘級，獲生口二千人，馬、牛、羊三萬餘頭，一種殆盡。

translations.literal: Again pursuing the fleeing rout, it happened that Shang and others by night were attacked by the Qiang; thereupon the righteous followers, Qiang and Hu, joined strength and defeated them, beheading in all before and after more than one thousand eight hundred grades, capturing two thousand living persons, more than thirty thousand head of horses, cattle, and sheep, and one tribe was nearly exhausted.
translations.idiomatic: In the pursuit Ren Shang's detachment was surprised by night. Deng Xun's sworn followers—Qiang and Hu together—counterattacked and killed over eighteen hundred in the two actions, took two thousand prisoners, thirty thousand head of stock, and all but wiped out that tribe.

sentences.zh: 迷唐遂收其餘部，遠徙廬落，西行千餘里，諸附落小種皆背畔之。

translations.literal: Mitang thereupon gathered his remaining following, moved his encampments far away, went west more than a thousand li, and all the attached lesser tribes turned their backs and rebelled against him.
translations.idiomatic: Mitang rallied his survivors, struck his tents, and withdrew a thousand li westward; every petty clan that had followed him peeled away.

sentences.zh: 燒當豪帥東號稽顙歸死，餘皆款塞納質。

translations.literal: The Burn-Dang great chief Donghao knocked his forehead in submission to await death; the rest all sincerely approached the passes and handed over hostages.
translations.idiomatic: The Burn-Dang leader Donghao came forehead to dust to offer his life; the others opened the frontier and delivered hostages in earnest.

sentences.zh: 於是綏接歸附，威信大行。

translations.literal: Thereupon he soothed and received those who submitted, and his awe-inspiring trust spread greatly.
translations.idiomatic: He welcomed every surrender with generosity, and his reputation for justice spread across the steppe.

sentences.zh: 遂罷屯兵，各令歸郡。

translations.literal: Thereupon he dismissed the garrison troops, each ordered to return to their commanderies.
translations.idiomatic: He then stood down the field army and sent the men back to their home commanderies.

sentences.zh: 惟置弛刑徒二千餘人，分以屯田，為貧人耕種，修理城郭塢壁而已。

translations.literal: Only he set more than two thousand convicts under relaxed sentence, divided them to till garrison fields, plow and plant for the poor, and repair city walls and stockade ramparts—that was all.
translations.idiomatic: He kept only some two thousand men on commuted sentences, split them among military colonies to farm for the destitute, and put them to patching walls and forts—nothing more.

sentences.zh: 永元二年，大將軍竇憲將兵鎮武威，憲以訓曉羌胡方略，上求俱行。

translations.literal: In the second year of Yongyuan the grand general Dou Xian led troops to garrison Wuwei; Xian, because Xun understood strategy toward Qiang and Hu, memorialized asking that he go together.
translations.idiomatic: In Yongyuan 2 Grand General Dou Xian took an army to Wuwei. Knowing Deng Xun's grasp of frontier policy, Dou petitioned to have him along.

sentences.zh: 訓初厚於馬氏，不為諸竇所親，及憲誅，故不離其禍。

translations.literal: Xun at first was thick with the Ma clan and was not intimate with the various Dous; when Xian was executed, therefore he did not fall into that calamity.
translations.idiomatic: Deng Xun had long been close to the Ma family, not to the Dou inner circle, so when Dou Xian died he escaped the purge.

sentences.zh: 訓雖寬中容眾，而於閨門甚嚴，兄弟莫不敬憚，諸子進見，未嘗賜席接以溫色。

translations.literal: Though Xun was broad at heart and tolerant of the multitude, in the inner quarters he was very strict; his brothers none did not revere and fear him; when his sons advanced to audience he never granted mats or received them with a warm look.
translations.idiomatic: Outwardly Deng Xun was easygoing, but at home he was austere: his brothers stood in awe of him, and when his sons came for an audience he never offered a seat or softened his expression.

sentences.zh: 四年冬，病卒官，時年五十三。

translations.literal: In the fourth year's winter he died of illness in office, at that time age fifty-three.
translations.idiomatic: He died in office in the winter of the fourth year, aged fifty-three.

sentences.zh: 吏人羌胡愛惜，旦夕臨者日數千人。

translations.literal: Clerks, people, Qiang, and Hu cherished and grieved for him; those who came morning and evening to mourn numbered in the thousands daily.
translations.idiomatic: Officials, commoners, Qiang, and Hu alike mourned him; thousands filed past the bier every day.

sentences.zh: 戎俗，父母死，恥悲泣，皆騎馬歌呼。

translations.literal: By Rong custom, when parents die they are ashamed to weep in grief; all ride horses singing and shouting.
translations.idiomatic: On the steppe it was thought shameful to wail for the dead; mourners instead rode in circles, singing at the top of their lungs.

sentences.zh: 至聞訓卒，莫不吼號，或以刀自割，又刺殺其犬、馬、牛、羊，曰：『鄧使君已死，我曹亦俱死耳。

translations.literal: When they heard Xun had died, none did not roar and howl, or with knives cut themselves, again stabbed to death their dogs, horses, cattle, and sheep, saying: 'Director Deng is already dead—we shall all die together.'
translations.idiomatic: When the word came that Deng Xun had died, they howled, slashed their own flesh, and slaughtered the dogs, horses, and sheep, crying that with the director gone they might as well die too.

sentences.zh: 』前烏桓吏士皆奔走道路，至空城郭。

translations.literal: Former Wuhuan officers and soldiers all ran on the roads, until cities and walls stood empty.
translations.idiomatic: Old Wuhuan troopers stampeded through the streets until towns looked deserted.

sentences.zh: 吏執不聽，以狀白校尉徐傿。

translations.literal: The clerks seized them and would not listen, reporting the situation to Colonel Xu Gui.
translations.idiomatic: Local officers tried to restrain them and sent word to Colonel Xu Gui.

sentences.zh: 傿嘆息曰：『此義也。

translations.literal: Gui sighed and said: 'This is righteousness.'
translations.idiomatic: Xu Gui sighed, 'Let them—they are showing true devotion.'

sentences.zh: 』乃釋之。

translations.literal: Thereupon he released them.
translations.idiomatic: He ordered them freed.

sentences.zh: 遂家家為訓立祠，每有疾病，輒此請禱求福。

translations.literal: Thereupon every household set up a shrine for Xun; whenever there was illness, they would come here to pray and seek blessing.
translations.idiomatic: Households raised shrines to him; the sick came to pray for a cure.

sentences.zh: 元興元年，和帝以訓皇后之父，使謁者持節至訓墓，賜策追封，謚曰平壽敬侯。

translations.literal: In the first year of Yuanxing, Emperor He, because Xun was the empress's father, sent an usher with credentials to Xun's tomb, granted a rescript posthumous enfeoffment, with posthumous title reverent marquis of Pingshou.
translations.idiomatic: In Yuanxing 1 Emperor He, honoring Deng Xun as the empress's father, dispatched an usher with imperial batons to his grave, issued a posthumous patent, and named him reverent marquis of Pingshou.

sentences.zh: 中宮自臨，百官大會。

translations.literal: The empress in person attended; the hundred officials held a great assembly.
translations.idiomatic: The empress came herself while the whole bureaucracy gathered for the rite.

sentences.zh: 訓五子：騭、京、悝、弘、閶。

translations.literal: Xun had five sons: Zhi, Jing, Kui, Hong, and Chang.
translations.idiomatic: Deng Xun left five sons: Deng Zhi, Deng Jing, Deng Kui, Deng Hong, and Deng Chang.

sentences.zh: 騭字昭伯，少辟大將軍竇憲府。

translations.literal: Zhi, courtesy name Zhaobo, in youth was summoned to Grand General Dou Xian's headquarters.
translations.idiomatic: Deng Zhi, courtesy name Zhaobo, began his career as a recruit in Dou Xian's command staff.

sentences.zh: 及女弟為貴人，騭兄弟皆除郎中。

translations.literal: When his younger sister became honored consort, Zhi's brothers were all appointed gentlemen of the palace.
translations.idiomatic: When his younger sister entered the palace as honored consort, all her brothers received appointments as gentlemen of the palace.

sentences.zh: 及貴人立，是為和熹皇后。

translations.literal: When the honored consort was established, this was Empress Hexi.
translations.idiomatic: When she was raised to empress, she became Empress Hexi, Empress Deng Sui.

sentences.zh: 騭三遷虎賁中郎將，京、悝、弘、閶皆黃門侍郎。

translations.literal: Zhi was thrice promoted to leader of court gentlemen for the rapid as tigers; Jing, Kui, Hong, and Chang were all gentlemen at the yellow gates.
translations.idiomatic: Deng Zhi rose thrice to leader of the rapid-tiger guard; Deng Jing, Deng Kui, Deng Hong, and Deng Chang became gentlemen at the yellow gates.

sentences.zh: 京卒於官。

translations.literal: Jing died in office.
translations.idiomatic: Deng Jing died in harness.

sentences.zh: 延平元年，拜騭車騎將軍、儀同三司。

translations.literal: In the first year of Yanping Zhi was appointed general of chariots and cavalry with ceremony equal to the three excellencies.
translations.idiomatic: In Yanping 1 Deng Zhi was named general of chariots and cavalry with honors matching the three dukes.

sentences.zh: 儀同三司始自騭也。

translations.literal: Ceremony equal to the three excellencies began from Zhi.
translations.idiomatic: The title 'equal to the three excellencies' began with Deng Zhi.

sentences.zh: 悝虎賁中郎將，弘、閶皆侍中。

translations.literal: Kui was leader of court gentlemen for the rapid as tigers; Hong and Chang were both palace attendants.
translations.idiomatic: Deng Kui led the rapid-tiger guard; Deng Hong and Deng Chang served as palace attendants.

sentences.zh: 殤帝崩，太后與步騭等定策立安帝，悝遷城門校尉，弘虎賁中郎將。

translations.literal: When Emperor Shang died, the empress dowager with chief ministers settled the plan to establish Emperor An; Kui was transferred to colonel of the gates; Hong was leader of court gentlemen for the rapid as tigers.
translations.idiomatic: When Emperor Shang died, the empress dowager and the ranking ministers fixed the succession on Emperor An. Deng Kui became colonel of the gates; Deng Hong took command of the rapid-tiger guard.

sentences.zh: 自和帝崩後，騭兄弟常居禁中。

translations.literal: From the time Emperor He died, Zhi's brothers constantly dwelt inside the forbidden precincts.
translations.idiomatic: After Emperor He's death the Deng brothers were seldom out of the inner palace.

sentences.zh: 騭謙遜不欲久在內，連求還第，歲餘，太后乃許之。

translations.literal: Zhi was modest and did not wish to remain long inside; repeatedly begged to return to his mansion; after more than a year the empress dowager then permitted it.
translations.idiomatic: Deng Zhi, uneasy at lingering inside, begged again and again to go home; only after a year did the empress dowager allow it.

sentences.zh: 其夏，涼部畔羌援蕩西州，朝廷憂之。
